holes.note: Longer hitters keep tee shot left to avoid fescue. Avoid going long on approach — collection area falls away behind green.
Solid tee shot can carry left bunkers for short approach. Front of green slopes away — land approach past front if pin is forward.
Green slopes right to left. Missed shot right of green leaves very difficult chip.
Lay up with fairway wood or hybrid short of the burn. Mid to long iron approach. Burn crosses fairway off tee.
Missing the fairway off the tee spells disaster. Good tee shot still leaves a long iron or fairway wood approach.
No need to cut the corner on this short par 4. Approach from left side of fairway gives better angle to green.
Any tee shot not on putting surface leaves an interesting chip. Read the green carefully — many slopes.
Keep tee shot down right side to avoid bunkers and leave best approach angle.
Long hitters can go for green in two but keep tee shot left to avoid fairway bunker. Lay-up decision required.
Play down right side to avoid tree. Green is very undulating — good first putt critical.
Keep tee shot down left side just short of three bunkers — still 200+ yards in.
Any mis-hit may end up in the burn. Hole often plays longer due to wind — pick one extra club.
Lay up with fairway wood or hybrid short of bunkers. Favour right side of green on approach.
Generous fairway but avoid bunkers scattered throughout. Green slopes front to back.
Green is bigger than it looks. Back half slopes away. Missing left leaves difficult up and down.
Tee shot must split the bunkers or expect a bogey. Front right bunker and undulating green demand accuracy.
Two options: lay up short of burn or carry the burn. Carry approach shot all the way.
Split the bunkers one last time — still a long approach into a long green. Limited margin for error.
